changes_summary: Added `onTick` callback to `createAutoScroller` (Step 1) that fires synchronously within the existing RAF tick after scroll mutation — each hook passes its state-update function as the callback to keep marquee bounds, drop indicators, and snap calculations current even when the pointer is stationary. Removed Step 5 (left-edge insets) as scope creep — not required for the core feature. Restructured Steps 2–4 to show exact `onTick` callback wiring per hook.
questions: Should the auto-scroll behavior differ between horizontal (time axis) and vertical (track axis)? Tracks are 36px tall so vertical speed may want to be lower.
Does the coordinator's editAreaRef need to be threaded into useExternalDrop as a new argument, or is it already accessible on the coordinator object?
success_criteria: Dragging a clip near any edge of the timeline scroll container causes the container to scroll in that direction.
Scroll speed accelerates as the pointer moves closer to the edge (not constant speed).
External file/asset/text-tool drops trigger auto-scroll when dragged near edges during dragover.
Marquee selection extends correctly when the scroll container auto-scrolls (selection rectangle grows to encompass newly-visible clips).
Auto-scroll stops immediately when the drag/drop/selection ends (no lingering scroll).
Auto-scroll stops when the pointer moves away from the edge zone back into the center.
When the pointer is stationary at an edge, drag indicators (drop position, snap, marquee rect) continue updating as the container scrolls — no stale state.
The drop-position indicator and snap calculations remain correct during auto-scrolling (no stale position bugs).
No visible jank or performance degradation during auto-scroll (single RAF loop, no duplicate timers).
assumptions: The existing `createAutoScroller` utility is the correct foundation — we extend its API with `onTick` rather than rewriting.
The `.timeline-canvas-edit-area` element is the single scroll container for both axes.
The DragCoordinator re-reads `getBoundingClientRect()` on each `update()` call, so calling it from `onTick` will see the new scroll position.
40px edge zone and 12 px/frame max speed are reasonable starting defaults — tuned after manual testing.

Left-edge insets for the sticky label column are not needed for v1 — the label column is inside the scroll container and `getBoundingClientRect()` reflects the visible area correctly. Can be added later if testing reveals issues.
